     The Numbers Business Report

Midway (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $56,846,802Details
International Box Office $69,940,558
Worldwide Box Office $126,787,360
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $12,067,847
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $19,697,035
Total $31,764,882
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The Battle of Midway, a clash between the American fleet and the Imperial Japanese Navy, marked a pivotal turning point in the Pacific Theater during World War II. The film, based on the real-life events of this heroic feat, tells the story of the leaders and soldiers who used their instincts, fortitude and bravery to overcome the odds.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$17,897,419 (31.5% of total gross)
Legs:3.18 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:44.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$100,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,242 opening theaters/3,242 max. theaters, 4.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $74,221,942

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 8th, 2019 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: November 7th, 2019 (Wide) (New Zealand)
November 7th, 2019 (Wide) (France)
November 7th, 2019 (Wide) (China)
November 8th, 2019 (Wide) (Netherlands)
November 8th, 2019 (Wide), released as Bitva u Midway (Czech Republic)
Video Release: February 4th, 2020 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of war violence and related images, language and smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2598 (Cert #52268), 10/2/2019)
Running Time: 138 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble
Plot point: Historical Battles, On a Boat, War
Social setting: In a Plane, Navy, World War II
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: 1940s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Providence Film Partners, Centropolis Entertainment, Entertainment One, Lionsgate, Shanghai Ruyi Entertainment, Beijing Universe Starlight Culture Media Company, Street Entertainment , AGC
Production Countries: United States, Canada
Languages: English

Synopsis

The Battle of Midway, a clash between the American fleet and the Imperial Japanese Navy, marked a pivotal turning point in the Pacific Theater during World War II. The film, based on the real-life events of this heroic feat, tells the story of the leaders and soldiers who used their instincts, fortitude and bravery to overcome the odds.
